concerns.text: The plan explicitly states: 'The project must either... use a simulated or synthetic fMRI dataset... acknowledging that scientific results will be illustrative.' and 'generate synthetic fMRI time-series data... to allow the rest of the pipeline to run.' This fundamentally breaks the research question. Investigating the 'impact of network centrality in ASD' requires real biological data to establish any valid association. Synthetic data cannot validate the hypothesis about ASD; it can only validate the code. The research question itself is unanswerable with the proposed data source.

The statistical approach (t-tests, FDR) is sound for real data, but the plan admits the data will be 'synthetic' with 'random noise' or user-defined effect sizes. Running t-tests on synthetic data to claim 'ASD group will exhibit altered network centrality' is a methodological fallacy. The results will be artifacts of the generator, not biological findings. The plan conflates 'pipeline validation' (testing code) with 'scientific investigation' (testing a hypothesis).

The plan claims to 'Complete preprocessing and analysis for ~100 participants per group' but the 'Research' section admits no real data exists in the verified list. The proposed 'Resolution Strategy' (Option A: Synthetic Data) creates a disconnect between the stated goal (investigating ASD) and the actual execution (testing a generator). The methodology does not support the spec's claim to investigate ASD centrality.
